summary: Backend and frontend smoke tested for CatalogForge (Italian). Auth (login/register/me/logout), Product CRUD, Catalog CRUD, upload, and full frontend nav flow (login → upload → products → catalog → history) all working. Product created via UI shows in grid; catalog listed in history.
passed: POST /api/auth/register new user -> 200 with cookies + /me works
POST /api/auth/logout -> 200 sends expiring Set-Cookie headers (verified in response)
Sidebar nav-upload/products/catalog/history all render and are clickable
Product upload form submits and toast 'Prodotto salvato' shown
Products grid renders 1 item after creation
Catalog page shows catalog-settings, catalog-name, tab-layout/style/content, slider-columns/rows, export-pdf/png/html, save-catalog
History page shows saved catalog in catalog-history-grid

failed.issue: PUT /api/products/{id} requires full body (name required); partial update payload {price:39.99} returns 422
failed.evidence: curl PUT with only price -> 422 missing 'name'

failed.issue: POST /api/products silently drops 'type' field; response stores product_type as empty string. Frontend field name may need to match backend model (product_type).

action_items: Optional: verify frontend PUT sends full product payload (it appears to since edit worked end-to-end).
Optional: align product 'type' field name — UI sends 'type' from input-type; backend expects 'product_type'. May cause type to be silently lost.
